PROSPECTUS SUMMARY

Acme Corp is a leading provider of cloud-based enterprise software solutions. We generate revenue through subscription licenses and professional services. Our platform serves over 5,000 enterprise customers across 40 countries.

RISK FACTORS

Investing in our common stock involves significant risks. You should carefully consider the risks described below before making an investment decision.

We have a history of net losses and may not achieve profitability. We incurred net losses of $142.3 million and $98.7 million in fiscal years 2024 and 2023, respectively.

We face intense competition. The market for enterprise software is highly competitive. Our principal competitors include Salesforce, Microsoft, Oracle, and SAP, all of which have greater financial resources than we do.

USE OF PROCEEDS

We estimate that the net proceeds to us from this offering will be approximately $450 million. We intend to use the net proceeds as follows: $200 million for research and development, $150 million for sales and marketing expansion, and the remainder for working capital and general corporate purposes.

BUSINESS

We were founded in 2015 with a mission to modernize enterprise data management. Our platform, AcmeCloud, provides real-time data synchronization, analytics, and workflow automation for Fortune 1000 companies.

COMPETITION

The enterprise software market is highly competitive. We compete primarily with legacy vendors such as SAP and Oracle, as well as newer cloud-native competitors including Snowflake and Palantir.

MANAGEMENT'S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION

The following discussion and analysis of our financial condition and results of operations should be read in conjunction with our financial statements and related notes.

Revenue increased 67% to $312.4 million for fiscal year 2024 compared to $186.9 million in fiscal year 2023. Subscription revenue grew 72% YoY to $285.1 million, representing 91% of total revenue.

Gross margin improved to 74% in fiscal 2024 from 68% in fiscal 2023. Operating loss was $(98.4) million versus $(76.2) million in the prior year.
